ingredients.name: Oarai Port
ingredients.localName: 大洗港フェリーターミナル
ingredients.address: Oarai Port, 大洗埠頭開発ビル 2 Minatochuo, Oarai, Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki 311-1305, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Oarai Port appears in Episode 10 as a destination on Youko's Ibaraki coastal route, the large ferry terminal now silent on a sea that no longer carries commercial traffic.

ingredients.name: Mitsumine Tea House
ingredients.localName: 大展望台 三峰茶屋
ingredients.entityContext: The Mitsumine Tea House viewpoint in Chichibu appears in Episode 12, Youko resting at the mountain tea house after a gruelling climb and looking out over the Okuchichibu mountain range.

ingredients.name: Yamabiko-so
ingredients.address: Yamabiko Ryokan, 6704 Manganji, Minamioguni, Aso District, Kumamoto 869-2402, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yamabiko Ryokan in Kurokawa Onsen, Kumamoto appears in Episode 12 as a warm stop that shows the series briefly venturing south to Kyushu, the traditional inn symbolising the hospitality of the world Youko has lost.

ingredients.name: Mt. Mitsumine
ingredients.address: Mitsuminesan, Misatomachi Nakano, Takasaki, Gunma 370-3111, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mt. Mitsumine in Gunma appears in Episode 12 as Youko climbs to a mountain summit with sweeping views of the North Kanto ranges, a physically demanding highlight of the final arc.

ingredients.name: Lake Shirakaba
ingredients.address: Lake Shirakaba, 白樺湖-3419-3 Kitayama, Chino, Nagano 391-0301
ingredients.entityContext: Lake Shirakaba in the Chino highlands appears in Episode 12, Youko arriving at the lakeside to find the birch-lined shore utterly quiet, the alpine scenery a fitting backdrop for the journey's closing chapters.

ingredients.name: Tosen Temple
ingredients.entityContext: Tosen Temple in Saitama appears in Episode 11 as a quiet waypoint on Youko's northern Kanto route, the small temple grounds offering a moment of calm reflection.

ingredients.name: Yoshimi Hyakuana
ingredients.address: Yoshimi Hyakuana, 327 Kitayoshimi, Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama 355-0155, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Yoshimi Hyakuana ancient burial caves in Saitama appear in Episode 11, Youko exploring the hillside honeycombed with historical pit tombs, the eerie setting perfectly matching the post-apocalyptic atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Tochigi–Gunma–Saitama border
ingredients.address: Tochigi, Japan

ingredients.name: Roadside station Kazo Watarase
ingredients.address: Roadside station Kazo Watarase, 1745-1 Onofukuro, Kazo, Saitama 349-1202, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kazo Watarase roadside station appears in Episode 11 as a rest stop for Youko along the Watarase River basin, its normally bustling parking lot now empty and overgrown.

ingredients.name: Tida Watarase Retarding Basin
ingredients.address: Watarase Yusuichi, 藤岡町藤岡, Tochigi, 323-1104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The vast Watarase Retarding Basin appears in Episode 11 as Youko rides along its embankments, the enormous flood-control wetland stretching to the horizon and reinforcing the sense of Japan returned to nature.

ingredients.name: Nikkō Tōshōgū
ingredients.address: Nikkō Tōshōgū, 2301 Sannai, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1431, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nikko Toshogu shrine is a centrepiece of Episode 11, Youko walking the ornate gate complexes in silence, the lavish Edo-period carvings and the absolute stillness creating one of the series' most haunting contrasts.

ingredients.name: Shinkyusha
ingredients.address: Shinkyūsha (Sanzaru, three monkeys), 2301-2 Sannai, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1431, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shinkyusha sacred stable at Nikko Toshogu appears in Episode 11, Youko spotting the famous three-wise-monkeys carving (see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil) on the empty building.

ingredients.name: Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa Memorial Park
ingredients.localName: 日光田母沢御用邸記念公園
ingredients.address: Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa Memorial Park, 8-27 Honcho, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1434, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa appears in Episode 11 as Youko explores the former imperial retreat, the elegant Meiji-Taisho era wooden halls standing silent and perfectly preserved.

ingredients.name: Kegon Waterfalls
ingredients.address: Kegon Waterfalls, 2479-2 Chugushi, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1661, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kegon Falls appears in Episode 10 as Youko reaches Lake Chuzenji and gazes at the famous 97-metre waterfall, one of the most iconic natural landmarks of the anime's Nikko arc.

ingredients.name: Kegon Waterfalls Paid Elevator
ingredients.localName: 華厳滝 観瀑台(エレベーター有料)
ingredients.address: Kegon Falls Observation Deck (elevator fee applies), 2479-2 Chugushi, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1661, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Youko takes the paid elevator down to the base-level observation deck at Kegon Falls in Episode 10, the close-up view of the crashing water a powerful sensory moment after a long ride.

ingredients.name: Anest Iwata Sky Lounge
ingredients.localName: アネスト岩田スカイラウンジ
ingredients.address: Anest Iwata Sky Lounge, 955 Kajiya, Yugawara, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 259-0313, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Youko stops at this Hakone Turnpike rest lounge to rest and survey the eerily quiet mountain road in Episode 1, one of the first scenic overlook moments that sets the lone-touring mood of the series.

ingredients.name: Tenkakudai
ingredients.address: Tenkakudai, 椿ライン Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0521, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This observation platform on the Hakone Turnpike appears early in Episode 1 as Youko pauses her ride to look out over the deserted Kanagawa coastline, a quiet moment that encapsulates the show's melancholy beauty.

ingredients.name: Hakonemachi(Bus)
ingredients.address: Hakonemachi-ko, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0521, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Hakonemachi port bus stop by Lake Ashi features in Episode 1, with Youko riding through the deserted lakeside town and noting the absence of any other travellers.

ingredients.name: Hakone Shrine 1st Torii Gate
ingredients.entityContext: Youko passes the lakeside first torii gate of Hakone Shrine in Episode 1, its iconic red pillars rising from the overgrown road as a reminder of the world that once was.

ingredients.name: 焼肉ISSA 柏店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒277-0005 Chiba, Kashiwa, 2 Chome−7−19 セナリオプレイス2 1F 焼肉ISSA 柏店

ingredients.name: Shimeki Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small store near Lake Ashi appears in Episode 1 as a relic of everyday commerce now abandoned, with Youko quietly noting what the pre-disaster world might have been like.

ingredients.name: The highest point of National Route 1 (874m)
ingredients.entityContext: The summit marker for National Route 1 at 874 m appears in Episode 1 as Youko crests the Hakone mountain pass, a milestone that fans recreate on motorcycle pilgrimages.

ingredients.name: Kinokuniya Ryokan
ingredients.localName: 美肌の湯 きのくにや旅館
ingredients.address: Kinokuniya Ryokan, ８番地 Ashinoyu, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0523, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kinokuniya Ryokan in Ashinoyu appears in Episode 1 as a once-welcoming hot-spring inn now standing silent, underscoring the series' theme of lost everyday warmth.

ingredients.name: Hakone Swing
ingredients.address: Mt. Taikan Observation Deck, 955 Kajiya, Yugawara, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 259-0313, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Daikanzan observation deck, nicknamed the Hakone Swing viewpoint, is featured in Episode 1 with a panoramic shot of Mt. Fuji and Sagami Bay that Youko takes in alone on her motorcycle.

ingredients.name: Hasedera
ingredients.address: Hase-dera, 3 Chome-11-2 Hase, Kamakura, Kanagawa 248-0016, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Youko visits Hasedera in Episode 2, the bamboo-lined path and the giant Kannon statue appearing in eerie stillness, making this one of the most memorable temple shots of the series.

ingredients.name: Yamashitacho IC
ingredients.entityContext: Youko enters Yokohama via the Yamashitacho interchange in Episode 2, riding through an overgrown bayside expressway that once carried heavy port traffic.

ingredients.name: Yokohama Bay Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Yokohama Bay Bridge features in Episode 2 as Youko rides across it alone, the massive cable-stayed span framing a panorama of Yokohama Harbour without a single ship or person.

ingredients.name: Sea Bass Pukarisanbashi Pier
ingredients.localName: ぷかりさん橋(みなとみらい桟橋·海上旅客ターミナル)
ingredients.address: Sea Bass Pukarisanbashi Pier, 1 Chome-1-地先 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Pukarisanbashi pier in Minato Mirai appears in Episode 2, Youko parking her bike to look out over the still, abandoned harbour, a poignant contrast to its normally busy ferry life.

ingredients.name: Anjindai Dai 2 Park
ingredients.address: Anjindai Dai 2 Park, 18-18 Anjindai, Yokosuka, Kanagawa 238-0048, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small Yokosuka park appears in Episode 2 during Youko's southbound leg, offering a quiet hilltop rest before she turns back north.

ingredients.name: Nakamuraya
ingredients.entityContext: This neighbourhood shop in the Todoroki area appears in Episode 3 as Youko scavenges for food, a tiny domestic scene that humanises the scale of the disaster.

ingredients.name: Todoroki Fudōson Temple
ingredients.address: Todoroki Fudōson Temple, 1 Chome-22-47 Todoroki, Setagaya City, Tokyo 158-0082

ingredients.name: Todoroki Ravine Park
ingredients.localName: 世田谷区立等々力渓谷公園
ingredients.address: Todoroki Ravine Park, 1 Chome-22-26 Todoroki, Setagaya City, Tokyo 158-0082
ingredients.entityContext: The forested ravine park in Setagaya is a key location in Episode 3, its lush greenery reclaiming the walkways as Youko explores what feels like a hidden valley lost in time.

ingredients.name: Koshindo
ingredients.address: Yasaka Kōshin-dō Temple, 390 Kinencho, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0828, Japan

ingredients.name: Doi Tax Accountant Office
ingredients.entityContext: This ordinary office building near Todoroki is spotted in Episode 3 as a background detail of the residential streets Youko passes, its everyday purpose now a reminder of the vanished working world.

ingredients.name: Handa Building
ingredients.address: Handa Building, 1 Chome-15-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Handa Building in Akihabara's Sotokanda district appears in Episode 4 as Youko explores an electronics district frozen in time, its shopfront signs still advertising goods no one will ever buy.

ingredients.name: Akiba's X Chuo
ingredients.localName: アキバのエックス 中央店
ingredients.address: Akiba's X Chuo, チチブ電機ビル 1F 3 Chome-12-15 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Akihabara hobby store appears in Episode 4 as Youko browses the silent aisles, finding shelves of anime merchandise and figurines left exactly as they were when the world ended.

ingredients.name: (Maid Cafe) at-home cafe Akihabara Main
ingredients.address: (Maid Cafe) at-home cafe Akihabara Main, Mitsuwa Building, 3-7F 1 Chome-11-4 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The at-home cafe in Akihabara is a bittersweet stop in Episode 4 as Youko steps into the empty maid cafe, imagining the lively atmosphere that once filled it.

ingredients.name: Niku no Mansei Akihabara Main Store
ingredients.localName: 肉の万世 アキバプレイス店
ingredients.address: Niku no Mansei Akiba Place, アキバプレイスビル 3F 3 Chome-15-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This longstanding Akihabara beef restaurant is passed by Youko in Episode 4 during her tour of the Electric Town district, its famously red sign still visible among overgrown streets.

ingredients.name: Beef Skewers Maruju Meat Shop
ingredients.localName: 牛串 丸十精肉店 秋葉原店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒101-0021 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−3−3 Beef Skewers Maruju Meat Shop, ドン・キホーテ秋葉原店
ingredients.entityContext: The Maruju yakiniku shop in Akihabara features in Episode 4 as Youko considers what she might cook if she could raid the larder of a world-famous beef district.

ingredients.name: MoriMori Store Akihabara
ingredients.localName: MoriMori Store & 森森買取 秋葉原駅前店 高価買取
ingredients.address: MoriMori Store Akihabara, チョムチョム秋葉原 1F 1 Chome-13 Kanda Sakumacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0025, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The MoriMori thrift store near Akihabara station appears in Episode 4 as Youko searches for useful secondhand goods, finding the aisles eerie in their abandoned orderliness.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Sta. Electric Town Exit West Square
ingredients.localName: 秋葉原駅 電気街口西側広場
ingredients.entityContext: The Electric Town exit plaza of Akihabara station is a key shot in Episode 4, Youko parking her motorcycle in a square that once teemed with otaku foot traffic but now echoes only with the wind.

ingredients.name: Kushiyoshi Ochanomizu
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒101-0021 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 2 Chome−1−6 法生ビル 1F 串よしお茶ノ水店
ingredients.entityContext: This Ochanomizu izakaya appears in Episode 4 as a neighbourhood dining spot Youko passes near the Akihabara district before heading further into central Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Juntendo University Hospital

ingredients.address: Juntendo University Hospital, 3 Chome-1-3 Hongo, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Juntendo University Hospital in Hongo appears in Episode 4 as Youko searches for medical supplies, the large teaching hospital now abandoned and silent.

ingredients.name: Suido Bridge
ingredients.address: Suido Bridge, 1 Chome-22-3 Hongo, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Suido Bridge over the Kanda River appears in Episode 4 as a road landmark Youko crosses during her Akihabara-to-Bunkyo circuit.

ingredients.name: Kinjotamahaka Park
ingredients.address: Kinjotamahaka Park, Imagami, Noda, Chiba 278-0025, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park in Noda, Chiba appears in Episode 5 as Youko passes through the northern Kanto suburbs on her way east, the empty park grounds marking her departure from central Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Ungao Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Ungao Bridge over the Tone Canal appears in Episode 5, a flat waterway crossing that Youko uses as a scenic waypoint through the Chiba flatlands.

ingredients.name: Yorogawa Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Yorogawa Bridge over the Yoro River in Chiba appears in Episode 5 as Youko heads toward the Boso Peninsula, the river glinting below in an otherwise silent landscape.

ingredients.name: Gorei Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Gorei Shrine on the Boso Peninsula appears in Episode 5 as a small, overgrown roadside shrine that Youko pauses at, a brief moment of reflection amid her solo ride.

ingredients.name: Tega Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Tega Bridge over Lake Tega appears in Episode 7 as Youko navigates the northern Chiba area, crossing the wide lake on an otherwise deserted road.

ingredients.name: Kashiwa-no-Mori Motorcycle Shrine
ingredients.localName: 柏の杜オートバイ神社
ingredients.address: Kashiwa-no-Mori Motorcycle Shrine, 770 Kaneyama, Kashiwa, Chiba 270-1455, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kashiwa-no-Mori Motorcycle Shrine in Kashiwa is visited in Episode 7, a shrine dedicated to safe riding that has special resonance for Youko as a lone motorcyclist in a dangerous world.

ingredients.name: Tone Drainage Pumping Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Tone drainage pumping station appears in Episode 7 as Youko rides the levee roads of the Tone River basin, the industrial infrastructure now unmanned and gradually rusting.

ingredients.name: Otone Bridge
ingredients.address: Otone Bridge, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Otone Bridge over the Tone River appears in Episode 7 as Youko crosses into Ibaraki, the wide river and empty bridge marking a geographic milestone on her journey north.

ingredients.name: Mizuho Village Market Place
ingredients.address: Mizuho Village Market Place, 496 Yagihashi, Tsukuba, Ibaraki 305-0842, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Mizuho Village Market in Tsukuba appears in Episode 7 as Youko discovers a farm produce market still stocked with goods, a rare hopeful moment where nature has kept things fresh.

ingredients.name: Gateway to Science
ingredients.entityContext: The Gateway to Science arch at Tsukuba Science City appears in Episode 7 as a symbolic landmark Youko passes while exploring a city built for the future that now stands empty.

ingredients.name: Science Expo Memorial Park
ingredients.address: Science Expo Memorial Park, 6 Miyukigaoka, Tsukuba, Ibaraki 305-0841, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tsukuba Science Expo Memorial Park appears in Episode 7, its retro-futuristic exhibition remnants and open lawns giving the post-apocalyptic world an oddly festive, overgrown quality.

ingredients.name: Welcia Tsukuba Ozone Store
ingredients.localName: ウエルシアつくば学園中央店
ingredients.address: Welcia, 24-6 Higashiarai, Tsukuba, Ibaraki 305-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Welcia drugstore in Tsukuba appears in Episode 7 as another supply stop for Youko, the fully stocked but staffless store exemplifying the series' survival-scavenging motif.

ingredients.name: Tsukuba EXPO Center
ingredients.localName: つくばエキスポセンター
ingredients.address: Tsukuba EXPO Center, 2 Chome-９ Azuma, Tsukuba, Ibaraki 305-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsukuba EXPO Center with its distinctive H-II rocket replica appears in Episode 7, Youko regarding the landmark with quiet awe as she tours the silent science city.

ingredients.name: Ayumizaki Park
ingredients.address: Ayumizaki Park, ４７８４番地地先 Saka, Kasumigaura, Ibaraki 300-0214, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ayumizaki Park overlooking Lake Kasumigaura appears in Episode 8, Youko gazing across Japan's second-largest lake from the park's headland viewpoint in an unusually serene moment.

ingredients.entityContext: The Odawara toll gate parking lot at the base of the Hakone Turnpike appears in Episode 1 as Youko's entry point to the mountain road, empty and overgrown without a single car.

ingredients.address: Yamane Park, 3 Chome-14 Suminoshindencho, Niihama, Ehime 792-0844, Japan

ingredients.name: Kanoya Imai Main Store
ingredients.address: Kanoya, Kagoshima, Japan

ingredients.name: Shoshimin Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small roadside store on the Hakone route appears in Episode 1 as Youko searches for supplies in an empty post-disaster neighbourhood, a quietly tense foraging moment.

ingredients.name: Beaver Tozan Odawara

ingredients.address: Beaver Tozan Odawara, 191-1 Itabashi, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Youko stops at this home-improvement store on the Odawara road in Episode 1, scavenging for useful gear in a world where ordinary shops now sit in silence.

ingredients.name: Eneos Odawara Itabashi SS

ingredients.address: Eneos Odawara Itabashi SS, ＥＮＥＯＳ小田原板橋ＳＳ 字中瀬-191 Itabashi, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This petrol station in Odawara features in Episode 1 as Youko refuels her motorcycle, one of the practical survival stops that punctuate her journey through abandoned Japan.

ingredients.name: Komine Okanenodai Ohorikiri Moat
ingredients.localName: 小峯御鐘ノ台大堀切東堀
ingredients.address: Komine Okanenodai Ohorikiri Moat, 3 Chome-30-27 Shiroyama, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic earthwork moat of Odawara Castle's outer fortifications appears in Episode 1 as Youko rides past, the ancient defences now doubly silent in a world without people.

ingredients.name: Fujimi Bridge
ingredients.address: Fujimi Bridge, 1 Chome-5 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Fujimi Bridge in Ariake, Tokyo appears in the opening animation sequence, its name meaning 'Fuji-view bridge' and its position amid the empty bayside district establishing the visual mood of the series.

ingredients.name: Hakone Shrine
ingredients.address: Hakone Shrine, 80-1 Motohakone, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0522, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hakone Shrine is a key stop on Youko's Episode 1 route, its forested approach and lakeside torii serving as one of the most visually striking post-apocalyptic pilgrimage moments in the series.

ingredients.name: First Torii Gate of Oarai Isosaki Shrine

ingredients.address: First Torii Gate of Oarai Isosaki Shrine, Isohamacho, Oarai, Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki 311-1301, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The first torii gate of Oarai Isosaki Shrine, standing in the surf of the Pacific, appears in Episode 10 as one of the most dramatic coastal images of the series, the red gate framed by an empty sea.

ingredients.name: Mitsumine Observatory
ingredients.address: Mitsumine Shrine, 298-1 Mitsumine, Chichibu, Saitama 369-1902, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mitsumine Shrine in Chichibu appears in Episode 12 as Youko ascends to the high-altitude shrine complex, one of the most dramatically situated sacred sites in the Kanto area and a fitting spiritual moment near the series' end.

ingredients.name: Starbucks Coffee - JR-East Hotel Mets Akihabara
ingredients.localName: スターバックス コーヒー JR東日本ホテルメッツ プレミア 秋葉原店

ingredients.entityContext: The Starbucks at JR-East Hotel Mets Akihabara is spotted in Episode 4 as Youko rides past the station precinct, the familiar green logo a poignant remnant of urban coffee culture.

ingredients.name: Horin Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-5-18 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Horin Park in Sotokanda appears in Episode 4 as a small pocket of green amid the Akihabara blocks, with Youko resting her bike and noting the contrast between nature reclaiming concrete.

ingredients.name: BicCamera AKIBA Store
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-1-１ Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The massive BicCamera electronics store in Akihabara appears in Episode 4, its many floors of gadgets and appliances sitting unsold and dark, embodying the series' theme of consumer civilisation frozen in time.

ingredients.name: Yashirobashi
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-17 Shinbashi, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yashirobashi bridge near the Todoroki area features in Episode 3 as a road-level view of Youko crossing through empty Tokyo suburbs on her motorcycle.

ingredients.entityContext: The Umihotaru rest area in the middle of Tokyo Bay Aqua-Line appears in Episode 5 as Youko crosses the bay, the artificial island offering a dramatic view of the water with no traffic in sight.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Station

ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Station's red-brick Marunouchi facade appears in the opening animation sequence, the vast rail hub completely empty, an image that crystallises the series' post-apocalyptic premise in a single iconic shot.

ingredients.address: 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Big Sight appears in Episode 3, its striking inverted-pyramid exhibition halls standing empty and overgrown in Ariake, a monument to a world that once held massive public gatherings.

ingredients.name: Togetsukyo Bridge

ingredients.entityContext: Togetsukyo Bridge in Arashiyama, Kyoto appears in Episode 10 as Youko crosses into the famous bamboo-grove district, the iconic bridge spanning an empty river beneath forested hills.

categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan

ingredients.entityContext: Youko visits the Tokyo Metropolitan Police Lost and Found Center in Episode 4 on the slim hope of finding useful items left behind, the building a small but memorable detour showing her resourcefulness.

ingredients.name: Rainbow Tower
ingredients.entityContext: The Rainbow Tower at Lake Kasumigaura appears in Episode 8 as a distinctive lakeside landmark Youko spots while riding the shore road, its coloured glass still glinting with no one to see it.

ingredients.name: Shin nakagawa Bridge
ingredients.address: Shin nakagawa Bridge, Motegi, Haga District, Tochigi, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shin Nakagawa Bridge near Motegi appears in Episode 8 as Youko heads toward the Twin Ring circuit, the river crossing marking her entry into the rural Tochigi countryside.

ingredients.name: Watanabe Shoji
ingredients.address: Rosana 201956 rosanahosokawa0920, 白鳳ビル 4F 5 Chome-9-24 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Mobility Resort Motegi North Entrance
ingredients.localName: モビリティリゾートもてぎ
ingredients.address: Mobility Resort Motegi, 120-1 Hiyama, Motegi, Haga District, Tochigi 321-3533, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Mobility Resort Motegi racetrack appears in Episode 8 as Youko rides through the pit lane and grandstands completely alone, the thrill of an empty professional circuit one of the show's most memorable sequences.

ingredients.name: Honda Collection Hall
ingredients.localName: ホンダコレクションホール
ingredients.address: Honda Collection Hall, 120-1 Hiyama, Motegi, Haga District, Tochigi 321-3533, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Honda Collection Hall inside Mobility Resort Motegi is explored in Episode 9, Youko wandering among historic Honda motorcycles and racing machines in a museum-silence that resonates deeply for a motorcyclist.

ingredients.name: Mito City Forest Park
ingredients.address: Mito City Forest Park, 588-1 Abokkecho, Mito, Ibaraki 311-4161, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mito City Forest Park appears in Episode 10 as Youko camps overnight in the trees, a rare moment of restful camping that gives her a chance to reflect on how far she has travelled.

ingredients.name: Oya History Museum - Historic Quarry Mine
ingredients.address: Oya History Museum - Historic Quarry Mine, 909 Oyamachi, Utsunomiya, Tochigi 321-0345, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Oya History Museum's massive underground quarry appears in Episode 10, Youko descending into the cathedral-like stone caverns and finding the scale of the carved-out space genuinely awe-inspiring even in the post-disaster world.

ingredients.name: Benkei's Sword-Split Rock and Turtle Rock
ingredients.localName: 弁慶の太刀割り岩・亀岩
ingredients.entityContext: The legendary Benkei's Sword-Split Rock and Turtle Rock at the Oya stone park area appear in Episode 10 as Youko explores the unusual rock formations surrounding the quarry.

ingredients.name: Koshiji Rock
ingredients.entityContext: Koshiji Rock near the Oya quarry appears in Episode 10 as Youko walks among the natural stone outcroppings, the area's geological character adding to the otherworldly feel of her tour.

ingredients.name: Kurokami-daira View Point
ingredients.localName: 黒髪平展望台(第二いろは坂)
ingredients.address: Kurokami-daira View Point, Hosoomachi, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1445, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kurokami-daira viewpoint on the Iroha slope road to Nikko appears in Episode 10, Youko pausing her climb to look back down the winding switchbacks stretching out below her.
